data.title: MPI Leads Gains As Tech And Energy Stocks Support Market
data.description:
The stock market ended the trading session on a mixed note, with semiconductor and energy-related counters providing support while weakness in consumer staples, plantations, and selected utilities weighed on sentiment.

At the closing bell, trading remained selective as investors continued to favour technology-linked stocks and defensive energy names. However, profit-taking in several large-cap counters limited broader market gains.

Leading the gainers was Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd, which rose 92 sen to RM47.76 on 210,000 shares traded, supported by continued interest in semiconductor-related counters. United Plantations Bhd followed, adding 30 sen to RM34.04 on 283,600 shares as plantation stocks saw selective buying interest.

Petronas Gas Bhd advanced 28 sen to RM17.68 on 400,900 shares, while KESM Industries Bhd gained 17 sen to RM4.25 on 204,300 shares, reflecting renewed interest in technology and manufacturing-related counters.

Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd also edged higher, rising 15 sen to RM8.05 on heavy turnover of 4,991,600 shares, making it one of the most actively traded gainers.

On the downside, Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd led the decliners, falling RM1.60 to RM91.52 on 1,504 shares, as selling pressure emerged in defensive consumer stocks. Hong Leong Industries Bhd slipped 44 sen to RM18.00 on 1,314 shares, while Batu Kawan Bhd declined 40 sen to RM20.90 on 137 shares.

UMS Integration Ltd eased 29 sen to RM7.90 on 10,500 shares, tracking weakness in selected technology counters. Tenaga Nasional Bhd fell 28 sen to RM14.28 on heavy trading of 4,830,600 shares, adding pressure to utility stocks.

Market analysts noted that the session reflected continued rotation into semiconductor and energy-linked counters, while profit-taking in consumer and utility heavyweights kept market breadth uneven. Activity remained concentrated in selected high-volume names, pointing to a cautious but selective trading environment.

data.title: FBM KLCI Slips 0.52% As Bursa Closes In The Red
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Wednesday, with the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) falling 9.00 points, or 0.52 per cent, to 1,711.37 at the close.

The benchmark index traded between an intraday high of 1,721.31 and a low of 1,709.15.

The broader market also finished in negative territory. The FBM Emas Index declined 53.80 points (0.42 per cent) to 12,655.96, while the FBM 70 Index eased 51.97 points (0.29 per cent) to 17,956.81.

The FBM Shariah Index shed 46.76 points (0.37 per cent) to 12,497.69, and the F4GBM Index fell 4.37 points (0.42 per cent) to 1,026.96.

Among the most actively traded counters, AIMAX gained 0.5 sen to 1.5 sen on volume of 358.08 million shares, followed by VS, which rose 1.0 sen to 26.0 sen. Zetrix slipped 1.5 sen to 71.5 sen, Tanco Holdings lost 1.0 sen to 30.5 sen, while Nexgram added 0.5 sen to 4.5 sen.

data.title: HSIF Bullish Trend Intact As RHB Eyes 25,300 Breakout
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its positive outlook on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF), saying the contract’s broader bullish structure remains intact despite a sharp pullback on Wednesday.

The research house said the HSIF fell 238 points to close at 24,916 after failing to sustain gains above the 25,100 level. The contract opened at 25,154, climbed to an intraday high of 25,181 before selling pressure pushed it to a low of 24,815, with the session ending near its lowest level.

During the evening session, the HSIF recovered 81 points and was last seen trading at 24,997.

Despite the weaker close, RHB Research noted that the contract continues to trade above its 50-day simple moving average (SMA), suggesting buyers still retain the technical advantage.

“As long as the HSIF holds above the key support at 23,500 pts, it may stage a rebound to resume the upside movement,” it said.

The research house said a decisive breakout above the immediate resistance at 25,300 points would improve market sentiment and could pave the way for a move towards the next resistance at 26,000 points.

Given the intact bullish structure, RHB Research advised traders to maintain long positions initiated at 24,657 points on July 15.

To manage downside risks, it recommended placing a stop-loss at 23,500 points.

RHB Research identified immediate support at 23,500 points, followed by 22,500 points, while resistance levels are seen at 25,300 points and 26,000 points.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Softer As Markets Eye FOMC And West Asia Conflict
data.description:
The ringgit opened slightly lower against the US dollar on Thursday, tracking weakness across major currencies as escalating tensions in West Asia and rising expectations of a US interest rate hike continued to support the greenback, Bernama reported.

At 8am, the local currency stood at 4.0855/0920 against the US dollar, compared with Wednesday’s close of 4.0850/0890.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said markets are increasingly focused on how the latest geopolitical developments could influence inflation and the US Federal Open Market Committee’s next policy decision.

“The odds for a September hike have risen to 64.8 per cent compared to 51.5 per cent in early July. Hence, it is going to be dollar-positive in the near term,” he told Bernama.

SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the ringgit had remained relatively resilient this week despite the broader strength of the US dollar, supported by Malaysia’s improving trade position and higher oil prices.

“The local currency has been supported by Malaysia’s improving terms of trade after the latest strong trade data, while its status as an energy exporter has also offered some relative protection as oil prices rise,” he said.

Against other major currencies, the ringgit strengthened against the Japanese yen to 2.5051/5092 from 2.5057/5081 at Wednesday’s close, but weakened against the euro to 4.6624/6698 from 4.6585/6631 and the British pound to 5.4648/4735 from 5.4612/4666.

The local currency was mixed against regional peers. It slipped against the Singapore dollar to 3.1646/1699 and the Thai baht to 12.0924/1173, while remaining unchanged against the Indonesian rupiah at 228.0/228.4 and the Philippine peso at 6.61/6.63.

data.title: Bursa Midday: Construction, Consumer And Energy Stocks Lift Market
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia maintained a cautious tone by midday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI holding slightly higher as gains in selected sectors helped offset weakness in broader market indices.

At 12.35pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 0.14 points or 0.01% to 1,711.51, after opening at 1,714.38. The benchmark index moved between an intraday high of 1,716.10 and a low of 1,709.05 during the morning session.

The broader market remained mixed, with the FBM Top 100 Index declining 6.94 points or 0.06% to 12,473.03, while the FBM Small Cap Index added 12.32 points or 0.08% to 15,783.39. The FBMEMAS Index slipped 5.88 points or 0.05% to 12,650.08.

Sector performance was led by construction stocks, with the Construction Index gaining 1.21 points or 0.42% to 287.44. The Consumer Products and Services Index climbed 1.02 points or 0.21% to 497.75, while the Energy Index rose 1.45 points or 0.19% to 775.86.

Meanwhile, the FBM70 Index eased 43.94 points or 0.24% to 17,912.87 as investors remained selective amid a lack of strong market catalysts.

Trading activity was dominated by AIMax, which recorded 2.43 billion shares traded, although the counter remained unchanged at RM0.015. Zetrix followed with 855.42 million shares traded, declining 1.5 sen to RM0.70, while VS Industry slipped 0.5 sen to RM0.255 after 815.03 million shares changed hands.

Among the top gainers, Pan Malaysian Industries advanced 26 sen to RM5.92, while Sunway Construction gained 17 sen to RM7.75.

On the losing side, Malaysian Pacific Industries fell RM1.44 to RM46.32, making it the biggest decliner, followed by United Plantations which dropped 84 sen to RM33.20. Petronas Gas also eased 16 sen to RM17.52.

data.title: SGX Opens Softer With Banking Stocks Under Pressure
data.description:
Singapore shares opened weaker on Thursday, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) falling 20.58 points, or 0.37%, to 5,574.84 in early trade as investors adopted a cautious stance.

As of 9.09am, market turnover stood at 95.59 million securities worth S$273.68 million. Market breadth was slightly positive, with 85 gainers outpacing 76 losers.

Banking counters traded lower, weighing on the benchmark index. DBS slipped 0.49% to S$73.30, while OCBC traded at S$28.92 and UOB at S$43.09.

Among other heavily traded blue chips, Seatrium changed hands at S$2.11, ST Engineering was at S$10.48, Singtel traded at S$4.43 and Sembcorp Industries stood at S$5.37.

In the derivatives market, trading volume reached 15,670 contracts in early dealings. The Nikkei 225 Index Futures for September traded at 67,035, while the MSCI Singapore Index Futures for July stood at 521.70. FTSE China A50 Index Futures for July were quoted at 15,156.

Other benchmark indices tracked by SGX included the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index at 1,534.67 and the iEdge S-REIT Index at 1,066.78.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Higher As Energy Stocks Lift FBM KLCI
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened slightly higher on Thursday, supported by gains in energy and consumer-related counters, although broader market performance remained mixed in early trade.

At 9.22am, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 1.62 points, or 0.09%, to 1,712.99 after opening at 1,714.38. The benchmark index traded between 1,711.01 and 1,716.10 during the opening session.

The broader market was mixed, with the FBM EMAS Index adding 5.92 points to 12,661.88, while the FBM Top 100 edged up 3.53 points to 12,483.50. The FBM Small Cap Index gained 47.05 points, or 0.30%, to 15,818.12.

However, the FBM 70 Index fell 30.34 points, or 0.17%, to 17,926.47, while the Bursa Malaysia Technology Index slipped 4.60 points, or 0.30%, to 1,542.76. The Bursa Malaysia Technology Services Index also eased 5.95 points, or 0.34%, to 1,720.48.

Among sectoral indices, the Energy Index led gains, rising 2.85 points, or 0.37%, to 777.26. The Consumer Products and Services Index advanced 1.22 points to 497.95, while the Construction Index added 0.46 points to 286.69.

Aimax remained the most actively traded stock with 182.04 million shares changing hands, followed by Velesto Energy, Kinergy Advancement, CEB Group and VS Industry.

Among gainers, Heineken Malaysia rose 16 sen to RM19.22, Hong Leong Industries gained 16 sen to RM18.16, while KESM Industries and Petrochemicals Nasional each added 12 sen to RM4.37 and RM4.84 respectively.

On the downside, United Plantations fell RM1.20 to RM32.84, while Malaysian Pacific Industries and Petronas Gas each declined 14 sen to RM47.62 and RM17.54 respectively.

data.title: Wall Street Slips As Tech Earnings, Oil Rally Keep Investors On Edge
data.description:
Wall Street ended mixed on Wednesday as investors turned cautious ahead of quarterly results from technology heavyweights Alphabet and Tesla, while a sharp rise in oil prices following renewed tensions in the Middle East added to inflation concerns.

The Nasdaq Composite led losses, falling 146.30 points, or 0.57%, to 25,690.90 as weakness in software stocks outweighed gains in semiconductor shares. The S&P 500 slipped 10.24 points, or 0.14%, to 7,498.96, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average edged down just 6.06 points, or 0.01%, to 52,218.58.

The market has rallied strongly since March on optimism surrounding artificial intelligence, but investors are now looking for evidence that billions of dollars invested by major technology companies are translating into earnings growth.

Second-quarter results from Alphabet and Tesla, the first members of the so-called “Magnificent Seven” to report this earnings season, were due after the closing bell.

“Investors have become a lot more discerning and specific as to where they’re choosing to invest in the AI trade,” said Kevin Gordon, head of macro research and strategy at Charles Schwab.

Alphabet closed 1.5% lower after its artificial intelligence ambitions came under scrutiny following a delay to a key AI model launch. Tesla ended 1.3% lower before extending losses to 3% in after-hours trading after reporting negative free cash flow for the second quarter, the first such result in more than two years.

Technology stocks delivered mixed performances, with the Philadelphia Semiconductor Index rising 0.4% for a third consecutive session, recovering from early losses after entering bear market territory last week.

Oil prices also remained a key focus after crude futures settled at their highest level since June 11, climbing around 3% amid renewed geopolitical tensions. Iran-backed Houthi forces in Yemen threatened shipping in the Red Sea, while US President Donald Trump vowed to destroy Iranian infrastructure if Iran attacked vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.

“Excluding the megacap AI trade, there’s an element of what’s going on with oil that’s driving the market,” Gordon said. “People are being defensive with utilities, but with energy and materials being higher, that’s the inflation component.”

Investors are also watching US monetary policy closely. According to a Reuters poll, economists expect the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged for the remainder of 2026, although many believe the risk of another rate hike remains elevated. Markets currently price a roughly 66% chance that the Fed leaves rates unchanged at next week’s meeting.

Among individual stocks, Super Micro Computer surged 19.8% after announcing more than US$60 billion in new fourth-quarter orders. The upbeat update also lifted Dell Technologies by 9.3% and Hewlett Packard Enterprise by 3%.

AT&T gained 3.5% after reporting stronger-than-expected wireless subscriber growth, while Philip Morris International rose 3.3% after exceeding quarterly earnings estimates on stronger cigarette demand.

Market breadth remained negative, with declining stocks outnumbering advancers on both the New York Stock Exchange and Nasdaq. Trading volumes were also lighter than average, with 15.16 billion shares changing hands compared with the 20-session average of 19.05 billion.

Reuters

data.title: US-Iran Tensions Lift Dollar While Yen Hovers Near 40-Year Low
data.description:
The US dollar held firm on Thursday as escalating tensions between the United States and Iran reinforced demand for safe-haven assets, while the Japanese yen remained close to its weakest level in nearly four decades as investors weighed the outlook for interest rates.

The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of major currencies, was little changed at 101.11 after gaining support from rising geopolitical risks and higher oil prices, according to Reuters.

Brent crude climbed more than 1.3% to US$95.31 a barrel after the US military launched another round of strikes on Iran. At the same time, Iran-backed Houthi forces claimed attacks on two Saudi oil tankers, raising concerns over disruptions to Red Sea shipping and global energy supplies.

Higher oil prices also pushed US two-year Treasury yields to a 17-month high on Wednesday, as investors grew increasingly concerned that inflationary pressures could strengthen the case for further Federal Reserve rate hikes.

“What is different from the start of the conflict five months ago is inventories. Lower inventories mean shortages of oil and gas are more likely the longer the conflict continues, exacerbating the negative economic impact of high energy prices which favours the USD,” said Joseph Capurso, head of international economics and foreign exchange at Commonwealth Bank of Australia.

The euro edged up 0.02% to US$1.1412 ahead of the European Central Bank’s policy meeting, where officials are widely expected to leave interest rates unchanged while signalling the possibility of another hike in September.

Meanwhile, the Australian dollar slipped 0.1% to US$0.6989, the New Zealand dollar eased nearly 0.1% to US$0.5811 and the British pound traded at US$1.3373.

The yen was little changed at 163.1 against the US dollar after briefly strengthening following a Bloomberg report suggesting Bank of Japan officials were open to raising interest rates more aggressively than markets had expected.

Reuters separately reported that the BOJ remains alert to upside inflation risks that could warrant faster monetary tightening.

The Japanese currency hit 163.23 on Tuesday, its weakest level since December 1986, reflecting persistent dollar strength and expectations that Japan’s interest rates will remain comparatively low.

“Against the backdrop of rising energy prices and mounting expectations of a more hawkish Fed meeting next week, it appears very unlikely — despite continued threats — that Japanese authorities will intervene until after next week’s FOMC meeting,” said Tony Sycamore, market analyst at IG Australia.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Picks: Ranhill Utilities And Dagang NeXchange
data.description:
HB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Ranhill Utilities Bhd and Dagang NeXchange Bhd (DNeX) as its latest technical trading ideas, citing strengthening bullish momentum following key resistance tests and breakouts.

RHB Research said Ranhill Utilities could extend its gains after testing the immediate resistance level with strong trading volume. The stock formed a long lower shadow in the previous session, indicating buying interest had emerged and bullish momentum was strengthening.

The research house said a decisive move above the RM2.05 resistance level could pave the way for further upside towards RM2.15, followed by RM2.25.

However, it noted that a fall below the RM1.95 support level would weaken market sentiment and could trigger a renewed correction.

Meanwhile, Dagang NeXchange is expected to maintain its upward trajectory after staging an upside breakout.

RHB Research said the counter registered fresh positive price action and closed above the RM0.46 resistance level, signalling that bullish momentum was gaining strength.

Following the breakout, the stock may advance towards the next resistance at RM0.50, with a further upside target of RM0.54, it said.

The research house added that a break below the RM0.42 support level would dampen market sentiment and open the door for a correction.

data.title: Sector Rotation Shapes Mixed Session with Utilities in Focus
data.description:
The stock market ended the trading session on a mixed note, with gains in utilities, construction, and selected plantation-related counters offset by selling pressure in semiconductor and heavyweight plantation stocks.

At the closing bell, sentiment remained cautious as investors rotated into infrastructure-linked and defensive names, while profit-taking in recent outperformers weighed on technology and consumer counters.

Leading the gainers was Panasonic Manufacturing Malaysia Bhd, which rose 33 sen to RM5.99 on 230,900 shares traded. Ornapaper Bhd followed, advancing 22 sen to 87 sen on 21,300 shares.

Tenaga Nasional Bhd also gained 22 sen to RM14.50 on heavy turnover of 7,400,700 shares, supported by renewed interest in utility stocks. Sarawak Plantation Bhd climbed 17 sen to RM4.49 on 472,700 shares, while Sunway Construction Group Bhd added 15 sen to RM7.73 on 3,437,200 shares, reflecting continued interest in construction-related counters.

On the downside, Malaysian Pacific Industries Bhd led the decliners, falling RM1.58 to RM46.18 on 139,900 shares, as semiconductor stocks faced profit-taking after recent gains. United Plantations Bhd slipped 94 sen to RM33.10 on 966,600 shares, tracking weakness in plantation counters.

Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd eased 32 sen to RM91.20 on 73,700 shares, while Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd declined 26 sen to RM20.66 on 154,000 shares. Malayan Cement Bhd fell 19 sen to RM6.14 on heavy trading of 4,021,000 shares.

Market analysts noted that the session reflected sector rotation, with investors shifting towards utilities and construction counters while locking in gains in technology and plantation names. Overall market activity remained selective, with high-volume trading concentrated in key industrial and infrastructure-related stocks.

data.title: US Stocks Mixed With Only Dow Rising While Nasdaq Sinks 0.3%
data.description:
US stocks diverged on Wednesday as investors looked ahead to earnings from Alphabet and Tesla  after the market close, while fresh tariffs and rising oil prices remained in focus.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average turned higher, rising 0.3%, and the S&P 500 hovered near the flat line. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite sank 0.3%, leading the retreat from a winning day on Wall Street the day before.

A tech recovery faces its next test later Wednesday when Alphabet and Tesla post second quarter results, the first two of the “Magnificent Seven” megacaps to report. Investors are weighing whether Google’s AI monetization efforts justify surging spending on AI. All eyes will be on Tesla’s capex, too, as the Elon Musk-led company vaults into its next phase of automation.

Yahoo Finance

data.title: Gold Eyes US$4,200 Resistance As Investors Track Fed Outlook
data.description:
Gold prices eased on Thursday after reaching a two-week high in the previous session, as rising oil prices linked to escalating Middle East tensions increased inflation concerns and raised expectations of a more cautious stance from the US Federal Reserve.

Spot gold fell 0.6% to US$4,103.39 per ounce by 0713 GMT, after climbing to US$4,165.87 on Wednesday, its highest level since July 7. US gold futures for August delivery also declined 1.1% to US$4,106.40.

The decline came as crude oil prices climbed to their highest level in more than six weeks following renewed military tensions involving the US, Iran and Yemen’s Houthis, which raised concerns over potential disruptions to global energy supplies.

Jigar Trivedi, senior research analyst at IndusInd Securities, said higher oil prices were adding pressure on inflation expectations and increasing the likelihood of future US rate hikes, limiting gold’s upside momentum.

The US dollar weakened 0.1%, making dollar-denominated gold more affordable for holders of other currencies. However, two-year US Treasury yields climbed to a 17-month high as investors assessed the impact of higher energy prices on inflation.

Markets are now focused on the Federal Reserve’s policy meeting next week, with the central bank widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged. Futures markets are pricing in a 77% chance of a rate increase in September, according to the CME FedWatch Tool.

Meanwhile, RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) noted that gold’s bullish momentum has strengthened after COMEX gold surged US$75.50 to close at US$4,151.90 in the previous session.

RHB Research said the commodity has been forming a series of higher lows, signalling improving momentum, with immediate support emerging at US$4,000 and resistance seen at US$4,200.

However, the research house maintained a bearish trading bias, expecting stronger resistance at US$4,400. RHB Research said it would retain its short position initiated at US$4,605.70, with a stop-loss level set at US$4,400.

The research house identified US$3,850 as the next downside support level if gold fails to hold above the US$4,000 mark.

data.title: Oil Prices Climbs To Six Week High To US$96
data.description:
Oil prices rose more than 1.5 per cent to their highest in more than six weeks on Thursday (Jul 23), with the United States launching a new round of strikes on Iran and Yemen’s Houthis targeting oil tankers in the Red Sea. 

Brent crude futures rose US$1.93, or 2 per cent, to US$96 by 12.11am GMT, the highest since Jun 8. It had settled over US$3 higher at US$94.07 in the previous session, just shy of a six-week high. 

US West Texas Intermediate crude rose US$1.44, or 1.7 per cent, to US$88.27 after rising nearly 3 per cent on Wednesday.

The US military said it carried out a 12th consecutive night of attacks on Iran hours after US President Donald Trump’s vowed to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant every time Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising the stakes in the war with Iran.Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said that an oil tanker had caught fire after an explosion while attempting to pass through what they described as a mined route south of the Strait of Hormuz, and that the two other tankers had turned back.

The Guards said the strait was under their control and “completely closed” while US actions continued in the region, warning that no tanker would be allowed to enter or leave without coordination with Iran.

As well as the renewed conflict over control of that key waterway, the Iran-aligned Houthis have opened a new front in the war by threatening to target vessels carrying Saudi oil in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait and announced a naval blockade of Saudi Arabia.

The Houthis said they had carried out a military operation targeting two Saudi oil tankers, and maritime security reports said one of the vessels named by the group, the Saudi-flagged tanker Encelia, had been hit in the Red Sea.

The Houthis said they had forced around 10 ships to retreat and return after warning vessels against sailing to Saudi ports. Reuters could not immediately verify this account.

The Houthis’ naval blockade on Saudi Arabia in the Red Sea threatens to disrupt global energy supplies beyond the Gulf, while Iran’s Revolutionary Guards’ spokesperson also warned shipping companies that the Strait of Hormuz southern route is mined in a post on X.

On the US supply side, crude stocks rose by 2 million barrels last week, the Energy Information Administration said, as refinery runs eased and crude exports dropped while imports rose. Analysts in a Reuters poll had estimated a 1.1 million-barrel draw.

Reuters

data.title: FBM KLCI Ends Higher On Selective Buying Despite Mixed Broader Market
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia closed on a mixed note on Wednesday, with the benchmark FBM KLCI rising on selective buying in heavyweight stocks, while broader market indices ended mostly lower.

The FBM KLCI gained 3.22 points, or 0.19 per cent, to close at 1,714.59.

However, the broader market was weaker, with the FBM70 Index falling 89.46 points, or 0.50 per cent, to 17,867.35. The F4GBM Index edged up 0.52 points to 1,027.48, while the FBM Emas Index added 1.77 points to 12,657.73. The FBM Shariah Index slipped 1.41 points to 12,496.28.

Among the most actively traded counters, AIMAX Bhd led the market with 3.30 billion shares changing hands, closing 0.5 sen lower at 1.0 sen.

Zetrix AI Bhd was the second most active, easing 0.5 sen to 71.0 sen with 1.21 billion shares traded.

Among other active stocks are Tanco Holdings Bhd,VS Industry Bhd, and Pegasus Heights Bhd.

The benchmark index’s modest gain contrasted with the softer performance across broader market gauges, reflecting continued selective buying amid cautious investor sentiment.

data.title: Euro Nears One-Week High Ahead Of ECB Decision As Oil Risks Cloud Inflation Outlook
data.description:
The euro climbed towards a one-week high on Thursday as investors awaited the European Central Bank’s (ECB) policy decision, with markets expecting policymakers to leave interest rates unchanged while signalling the possibility of another hike later this year amid rising energy prices.

The single currency rose 0.17% to US$1.1429, while the US dollar index, which tracks the greenback against a basket of major currencies, eased 0.13% to 100.98.

Although the ECB raised interest rates in June and indicated further tightening could follow, recent data showing easing inflation, slower wage growth and softer economic activity have reduced expectations of an immediate follow-up increase.

However, escalating tensions in the Middle East have pushed oil prices to their highest level in more than six weeks, raising concerns that higher energy costs could reignite inflationary pressures across the eurozone.

“We have flagged a small risk that the ECB decides to deliver an early hike,” said Samara Hammoud, international economist and currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia. “A ‘hawkish hold’ is another upside risk to EUR/USD today.”

Brent crude extended gains after the US launched fresh strikes against Iran while Iran-backed Houthi forces claimed attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea, adding to fears of supply disruptions and complicating the inflation outlook for central banks.

Elsewhere, commodity-linked currencies also strengthened, with the Australian dollar rising 0.23% to US$0.7012 and the New Zealand dollar trading at US$0.5818. Sterling gained nearly 0.1% to US$1.3384.

In cryptocurrency markets, Bitcoin slipped 0.5% to US$65,557.35, while Ether fell 0.44% to US$1,917.65.

The Japanese yen showed little sign of recovery, edging just 0.04% higher to 163.05 against the US dollar after touching 163.23 earlier this week, its weakest level since December 1986.

A Reuters survey published on Thursday found that 86% of economists expect the Bank of Japan (BOJ) to raise interest rates again this year, potentially as early as October, although 95% believe rates will remain unchanged this quarter.

Japan’s Finance Minister reiterated on Thursday that authorities stand ready to take decisive action in the foreign exchange market if necessary, following repeated verbal interventions as the yen remains under pressure.

Tokyo previously conducted yen-buying interventions in April and May after the currency weakened beyond the 160-per-dollar level.

The Reuters poll also showed nearly 80% of economists believe the yen’s current level around 160 per US dollar is weaker than justified by Japan’s economic fundamentals.

Kumiko Ishikawa, senior foreign exchange market analyst at Sony Financial Group, said the dollar’s move above the 163 level has heightened market expectations of intervention, although the absence of stronger warnings from Japanese officials has left traders uncertain over when authorities might step in.

Reuters

data.title: Asian Chipmakers Rally As Alphabet Raises AI Spending Plans
data.description:
Asian equities advanced on Thursday after major US technology companies reaffirmed aggressive spending on artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure, lifting regional chipmakers, while escalating conflict in the Middle East pushed oil prices to their highest level in six weeks.

Investor sentiment was buoyed after Alphabet and Tesla reported earnings that reinforced expectations of sustained AI infrastructure investment, with Alphabet raising its capital expenditure forecast for 2026.

The increased spending is expected to benefit Asian semiconductor manufacturers, with South Korea’s KOSPI surging more than 3% on gains in SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics, while Japan’s Nikkei climbed 0.7%.

MSCI’s broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan gained about 1%, putting it on track for a 3% weekly gain and snapping a two-week losing streak.

Gary Tan, portfolio manager at Allspring Global Investments, said stronger cloud growth validates higher AI capital expenditure and reinforces that the hyperscaler investment cycle still has momentum.

However, analysts cautioned that investors are increasingly focused on whether the enormous AI spending by US technology giants will translate into meaningful returns.

“For markets, this is not an AI-demand problem, it is an AI-return problem,” said Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo in Singapore.

She noted that while US megacap technology firms bear the cost of AI investments, chipmakers, memory suppliers and infrastructure companies are likely to benefit earlier in the investment cycle.

Meanwhile, European stock futures pointed to a subdued open ahead of the European Central Bank’s policy decision later on Thursday. The ECB is widely expected to keep interest rates unchanged while retaining the option of raising rates in September amid heightened geopolitical risks.

Nasdaq futures edged down 0.1% as investors awaited earnings from Microsoft, Meta Platforms and Amazon next week.

Oil prices extended gains after the US launched another round of strikes against Iran and Yemen’s Houthi forces targeted oil tankers in the Red Sea, intensifying concerns over supply disruptions.

Brent crude rose 2% to US$96 a barrel, its highest level in six weeks.

Analysts warned that any disruption to shipping through both the Strait of Hormuz and the Bab el-Mandeb Strait could affect more than a quarter of global oil and gas supplies, raising concerns over inflation and global economic growth.

“The worries about global growth are well-placed, we think,” said Thierry Wizman, global foreign exchange and rates strategist at Macquarie Group.

Higher oil prices also fuelled expectations that the US Federal Reserve could tighten monetary policy further, with traders pricing in 42 basis points of rate hikes this year and a September increase now fully priced.

In currency markets, the Japanese yen traded at 163.07 against the US dollar after Bloomberg reported that Bank of Japan officials were open to a faster pace of rate hikes.

The currency had weakened to 163.24 earlier this week, its lowest level since December 1986, keeping traders alert for possible intervention by Japanese authorities.

OCBC strategists said while foreign exchange intervention could slow the yen’s decline, a more lasting recovery would likely require the Bank of Japan to accelerate interest rate increases.

Reuters

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates July 23, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on July 23, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

data.title: US Stock Market In The Red On Tech Earnings, Surging Oil Prices
data.description:
US stocks fell on Thursday as investors digested the latest spending outlooks from Alphabet and Tesla, and eyed surging oil prices in the wake of expanded attacks in the Middle East.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average and the S&P 500 dropped by 0.9%. The tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite declined by 1.9%, adding to Wednesday’s stock retreat amid a flurry of earnings reports.

Big Tech stocks were under pressure on Thursday morning, led by declines in shares of Alphabet and Tesla after the two “Magnificent Seven” heavyweights reported results after Wednesday’s market close.

Alphabet posted a strong quarter fundamentally, but the Google parent’s raised capex outlook comes as investors scrutinize AI’s return on investment. Tesla CEO Elon Musk also said 2026 would be a “massiveWall Sy capex year” for the company, highlighting a focus on Optimus robots, robotaxis, and data centers.

data.title: Tokyo Stocks Closes Higher On Tech Exuberance
data.description:
Tokyo stocks closed higher on Thursday, supported by gains in technology shares.

The benchmark Nikkei stock index, the 225-issue Nikkei Stock Average, ended up 307.00 points, or 0.46 percent, from Wednesday at 66,422.60.

The broader Topix index, meanwhile, finished 20.75 points, or 0.51 percent, higher at 4,053.88.

On the top-tier Prime Market, mining, bank and nonferrous metal issues were notable gainers.

Analysts said some heavyweight semiconductor and artificial intelligence-related shares rose after undergoing a correction phase, buoyed by signs of sustained strong demand for AI infrastructure in the United States.

However, the market’s upside was limited as rising Japanese government bond yields and higher crude oil futures fueled concerns over their potential impact on corporate earnings
